chunk_summary: Guidelines for MAM follow-up visits include documenting anthropometry, feeding, morbidity, and supplement use, with escalation criteria for complications or weight loss.
learning_objectives: Understand the essential components of a follow-up visit checklist for MAM management.
Identify criteria for escalation and referral during MAM follow-up.
Learn how to assess and document anthropometric and clinical data in MAM cases.
Recognize the importance of WHO high-risk criteria in guiding follow-up frequency and referrals.
key_facts: Anthropometric measures include weight, height/length, MUAC, and WHZ/WLZ, with repeat measurements if borderline.
Immediate referral is required for nutritional oedema, danger signs, or clinical deterioration.
Weight loss or falling MUAC during follow-up is a red flag requiring reassessment and possible referral.
State protocols recommend using complications or weight loss to trigger referral to Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRC).
WHO high-risk criteria suggest more frequent contact and lower thresholds for investigation and referral.
reference_quote: State protocols summarized in Indian compendia explicitly use complications or weight loss to trigger referral to facility-based centres such as Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRC).
document_key_topics: Role of WHO high-risk criteria in MAM follow-up
